page: Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine belonging to the Dnlv6 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated matrix operations.

This server type does not include local storage, though it is premium storage capable, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. The low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU represents a clear tradeoff, making the instance less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large in-memory datasets. However, the hardware profile is optimized for network-heavy workloads, leveraging its dedicated compute resources and high-frequency Intel Xeon cores to process network traffic efficiently.

In benchmark testing, the D48nls_v6 demonstrates strong performance across several compute and memory metrics. Its Passmark CPU score of 56283.8 and Passmark CPU extended instructions test score of 54193.39 are categorized in the strong tier. Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong read speeds of 252.33 GB/sec and write speeds of 104.24 GB/sec. The instance also exhibits strong capabilities in static web serving throughput at 73.21 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 275.3 GB/sec. For small-model LLM inference, it achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 337.85 tokens/sec, while medium-model text generation remains strong at 29.73 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48nls_v6 offers a high-density compute profile with a lower memory allocation, reducing the overall cost overhead typically associated with larger memory footprints. This makes it a cost-effective option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive RAM arrays. Recommended workloads include network virtual appliances, web serving, multi-threaded compression tasks, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnlv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage but supports premium storage options. It excels in compute-heavy and network-intensive tasks, as evidenced by strong benchmark results, including a Passmark CPU score of 56283.8 and a static web serving throughput of 73.21 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 337.85 tokens/sec for small LLM models. The qualitative cost-efficiency is high for compute-focused workloads due to the reduced memory overhead. This server is best suited for network virtual appliances, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and strong compute benchmarks, making it ideal for web serving and network appliances.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, built for compute-heavy and web serving workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized compute instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and low-memory density for efficient, high-throughput web serving and processing.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores.
Features 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage options.
Delivers strong benchmark performance in static web serving and small-model LLM inference.
Optimized for network virtual appliances, web serving, and compute-bound workloads.
